response.text:
{
  "consensus": [
    "All three seats produce a deterministic, ordered verification plan built from pure transcript-string accept/reject predicates rather than nondeterministic timing or network checks.",
    "All seats treat the sole supplied mechanical evidence (`$ regression-test` / `one parser regression test passed` / `[exit 0]`) as a positive baseline only, not as proof of nonzero rejection or echo-literal ignore.",
    "All seats require synthetic paired vectors: structural nonzero markers (e.g. trailing `[exit 1]`) must reject; literal `exit 1` inside echoed command/output with a true zero trailer must accept; mixed cases give precedence to the structural marker.",
    "All seats preserve malformed transcript boundaries as an explicit non-blocking residual/minority risk that green happy-path vectors do not discharge.",
    "All seats disclaim seat-side workspace access or execution beyond the supplied evidence and frame the plan as host-applied inputs/expected outcomes for a dependency-free parser."
  ],
  "contradictions": [
    "Boundary policy: Seat B defaults to fail-closed rejection on malformed/truncated/split markers; Seat A explicitly warns against treating truncated `[exit 1` as a nonzero exit (prefer ignore/soft-fail over inventing failure); Seat C leaves malformed/absent-marker behavior more open (reject or unknown per contract) while calling for future hardening.",
    "Host/workspace scope: Seats A and C keep the plan as self-contained string oracles with no filesystem/inventory claims; Seat B requires host-side plugin source/manifest enumeration, write-once fixture corpus, regression re-run that includes new fixtures, and a frontier-only release-smoke evidence pack—closer to operational CI than the task’s “no workspace access or execution beyond supplied evidence” constraint.",
    "Dependency-free proof: Seat B gates on a static dependency inventory from source/manifest; Seats A and C treat dependency-freedom as a plan constraint (stdlib/string-only checks) without requiring a host inventory step that the supplied evidence cannot support.",
    "Marker grammar lock: Seat A recommends strict exact `[exit N]` (N=0 accept, N>0 reject; variants like `[exit 00]`, case, whitespace as non-markers or malformed); Seats B and C leave more room for bare status lines, alternate phrasings, or host-observed grammar."
  ],
  "partial_coverage": [
    "Literal-ignore vectors are specified by all seats but differ in breadth (A: multiple echo/false mixes and nested transcript-like text; B: echo/printf plus non-echo contexts like sed/here-docs and payload-fake `[exit 0]` text; C: minimal echo and simulate variants).",
    "Nonzero rejection is universally required but coverage of codes (1 vs 2/130/255), bare vs bracketed forms, and multiple-marker precedence (last wins vs any-nonzero rejects) is unevenly locked.",
    "Absent-marker and empty-transcript policy is mentioned as uncertainty by all but only partially fixed in steps (success-by-default vs reject vs unknown).",
    "Determinism mechanics (fixed order, Boolean/structured results, idempotence, exact-match vs regex anchors) appear in all plans but with different completeness of pass/fail reporting requirements."
  ],
  "unique_insights": [
    "Seat A: Adversarial catalog including Unicode/homoglyphs, escaped forms, streaming split markers, recursive nested “transcript-looking” echoed text, and an explicit precedence ordering (structural markers before literal-ignore rules).",
    "Seat B: Constraint-audit table mapping each stated requirement to evidence gaps; frozen named fixtures (`T_ok_echo`, `T_rej_marker`, `T_payload_fake`, `T_boundary_*`); predicate table and fixture-hash evidence pack under a named frontier-only default profile.",
    "Seat C: Minimal trailer-only cases and an optional pure assertion that mutating only the final marker flips accept→reject while inserting earlier literal `exit 1` does not—compact oracle clarity."
  ],
  "minority_findings": [
    "Malformed transcript boundaries are the mandated minority residual across all seats: truncated/partial markers, missing final newline, split/fused transcripts, dual conflicting trailers, and non-UTF8/control bytes can cause false accept or false reject; well-formed `[exit 0]` evidence does not resolve this.",
    "Seat A’s supported lone emphasis: do not false-reject by inventing a nonzero exit from garbage/truncated markers; document boundary behavior explicitly even if the core suite passes.",
    "Seat B’s supported lone emphasis: keep fail-closed as default safety posture, mandate a dedicated boundary negative test and release-note residual, and never silently repair broken boundaries.",
    "Seat C’s supported lone emphasis: future hardening options (single canonical marker in final N lines, reject conflicting trailers, length/checksum sidecar) without blocking the current plan."
  ],
  "blind_spots": [
    "No seat is given the actual parser implementation, so all plans assume a pure function over whole-transcript strings and cannot verify real code paths, streaming APIs, or error object shapes.",
    "Exact on-wire grammar of “actual nonzero exit marker” remains underspecified beyond the single `[exit 0]` example; correlated assumption risk if the real plugin uses a different trailer dialect.",
    "Dependency-freedom cannot be evidenced from the supplied transcript alone; any strong “zero deps proven” claim exceeds mechanical evidence.",
    "Limited treatment of encoding (CRLF vs LF), binary/NUL payloads, and extremely large inputs as systematic reject/accept policies.",
    "No seat fully resolves whether command-prefix/`$` validation is in scope versus exit-marker scanning only."
  ],
  "final_guidance": [
    "Author a single host-executable deterministic verification plan (not a vote tally) whose core is ordered, self-contained transcript vectors with exact expected accept/reject outcomes: (1) supplied `[exit 0]` baseline accept; (2) structural nonzero markers reject; (3) echoed literal `exit 1` with zero trailer accept; (4) mixed literal+nonzero reject via structural precedence.",
    "Lock explicit, testable rules in the plan text: marker form (recommend exact trailing/lone-line `[exit N]`), N=0 vs N≠0, multiple-marker precedence, and non-marker forms (mid-line, unbracketed, case/whitespace variants).",
    "Embed the synthetic vectors as string literals in the plan; state that only the host applies them to the parser; do not claim seat workspace access, live plugin execution, or evidence beyond the supplied block plus those embeddings.",
    "Preserve—do not dilute—the minority residual on malformed transcript boundaries; require documentation/tests of truncated, split, duplicate, and absent markers; prefer a stated policy that avoids inventing nonzero status from garbage while still recording the residual risk after green happy-path results.",
    "Treat Seat B’s operational extras (manifest inventory, frontier-only smoke, hash packs) as optional host-side hardening only if they do not violate the no-extra-evidence/workspace constraint; do not make unsupplied CI profile machinery a hard gate of the plan itself.",
    "Surface remaining uncertainties (bare status lines, absent-marker default, exact reject signaling) as fixed plan choices rather than silent assumptions, and require the final report to note that the single mechanical `[exit 0]` line does not by itself prove nonzero rejection or echo-ignore."
  ]
}
